title: × Laeliocattleya veitchiana
taxonomy: × Laeliocattleya veitchiana is a hybrid orchid, specifically a cross between Laelia and Cattleya orchids. It belongs to the family Orchidaceae and is a part of the subtribe Laeliinae. This orchid is further classified as a natural hybrid, resulting from the natural cross-pollination of its parent species. The classification of × Laeliocattleya veitchiana is a combination of its parent genera, which is a common practice in horticulture for naming hybrid orchids. The classification system used is based on the International Code of Nomenclature for algae, fungi, and plants (ICN).

intro: The plant × Laeliocattleya veitchiana, also known as the Veitch's Cattleya, is a hybrid orchid species that belongs to the family Orchidaceae. This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering potential health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, as well as antioxidant and immunomodulatory activities, which may aid in the treatment of various conditions. The bioactive constituents of × Laeliocattleya veitchiana include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, the plant is often used to prepare herbal teas, tinctures, and infusions, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
